symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden-onset severe pelvic cramping, now largely resolved

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid spontaneous easing with rest and no red-flag features favours a benign musculoskeletal/GI/functional cause.
Sudden severe pelvic pain in a reproductive-age person keeps ectopic on the list until pregnancy is excluded.
Abnormal bleeding is the cardinal symptom of a symptomatic cervical malignancy; its absence lowers concern and keeps this a screening (not diagnostic) pathway.
Absence of bleeding modestly lowers, but does not exclude, ectopic pregnancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever or abnormal/foul-smelling discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PID typically features fever and/or abnormal discharge; their absence argues against active pelvic infection.
Being overdue is the actionable issue and the indication for the screening pathway.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reproductive-age person with a cervix; pregnancy status not established

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pregnancy must be excluded (test / in-person beta-hCG) before acute pelvic pain is attributed to a benign cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Macroscopically normal cervix on speculum examination (no friable/exophytic mass, no purulent discharge)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A macroscopically normal cervix argues strongly against a visible invasive cancer — but this is a clinician-only speculum finding, sealed and unavailable to the AI Doctor remotely.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Failure to exclude pregnancy leaves ectopic pregnancy unaddressed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative beta-hCG effectively excludes ectopic pregnancy — but the AI cannot obtain this remotely; a home urine pregnancy test can be advised, and in-person confirmation arranged.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cervical HPV (nucleic-acid) screening sample from the transformation zone

investigation_details.investigation_name: Cervical Screening Test — primary HPV test
investigation_details.expected_result: Detection or absence of high-risk HPV DNA (result pending)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The overdue screening remains undone; persistent oncogenic HPV could go undetected.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The HPV test is the screening itself; it must be collected in person or via an approved self-collection pathway, never performed by the AI Doctor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs within normal limits (afebrile, haemodynamically stable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal, stable clinician-measured vitals support clinical stability — but these are clinician measurements, sealed and not obtainable by the AI without a connected device.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Cervical Screening Test (primary HPV test) — arranged in person or via approved self-collection
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The overdue screening is the core actionable issue; it must be arranged through a provider, not performed by the AI.
Exclude pregnancy/ectopic before attributing acute pelvic pain to a benign cause; can be advised as a home test with in-person confirmation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate emergency imaging / transvaginal ultrasound for acute pelvic pain
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated by a resolved cramp with a negative red-flag screen; over-ordering emergency imaging reflects anxiety-driven over-triage.
